observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to create an
Inventory Committee that will spearhead and monitor the implementation
of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE.

2.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to thoroughly
review and reconcile the transactions that resulted in the
abnormal/negative balance of the Due to BIR account and accordingly
prepare the adjusting entries to establish the accurate balance thereof.

3.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to submit a
written request for write-off of the Due from LGUs account pursuant to
the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

4.8 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to reconcile their records to address the noted
discrepancy of ₱13,625.03, make the necessary adjustments and conduct
the same quarterly.

5.6 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Require each collector to remit collections with corresponding
reports at the end of the workday;
b. Deposit the collections intact daily or not later than the next banking
day; and
c. Monitor strict compliance of collectors with these requirements, and
promptly document and address any delays or discrepancies.

6.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare a
Disbursement Voucher in every remittance of taxes withheld monthly to
the BIR.

7.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prioritize immediate
reconciliation with GSIS to address the outstanding balance and
implement a monitoring system to ensure that all future premium
payments are made on time and in full.

8.6 We recommended that the:
8.6.1.1 Payroll-in-Charge discontinue the remittance of Pag-IBIG loans
and savings for employees not included in the payroll deduction
and advise employees whose salary is below the ₱5,000.00
minimum net-pay threshold to pay directly to Pag-IBIG branch; and
8.6.1.2 Concerned employee submit the official receipts issued by the
MTO for the payments made on loans and MP2 savings from
August 2024 to December 2024.

9.10 We recommended that the:
9.10.1 Payroll-in-charge personnel withhold the monthly PhilHealth
premium contributions from the salary of the Municipal Mayor;
and
9.10.2 Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments and remit
the unwithheld premium contributions of the Municipal Mayor for
CYs 2016 to 2024 to PhilHealth immediately to avoid the imposition
of penalties.

10.7 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to all
Implementing Units of the projects, programs and activities of the 20%
DF to refrain from charging expenses or disbursements that are
administrative in nature to reduce, if not eliminate the risk of recurrence.

11.8 We recommended and the HRMO II agreed to execute a contract of
service in hiring a JO worker, detailing the wage rates, duties, expected
outputs, and the terms of engagement and require the JO workers to
prepare DTRs and accomplishment reports to support the payroll claim,
as well as any other terms and conditions necessary for their job.

12.5 We recommended and the LDRRM Officer agreed to refrain from using the
LDRRMF for expenses not directly related to disaster risk reduction and
management to fully maximize the fund.

13.9 We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to refrain from using
the GAD Fund for expenses not related to addressing gender issues,
promotion of gender equality, and providing of opportunities for women’s
empowerment.

14.9 We recommended that the:
14.9.1 Municipal Treasurer effect the available remedies for collection of
RPT and SET that have been long outstanding; and
14.9.2 Management allocate budget for the massive land tax mapping to
be conducted by the Municipal Assessor to cleanse and update
their records and come up with accurate list of RPT/SET
collectibles.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly abide by the applicable accounting rules and regulations, especially in the classification of the Local Government account and the recording thereof in the proper specific appropriate expense account, to avoid the presentation of misleading information in the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: 1.12 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly abide by the applicable accounting rules and regulations, especially in the classification of the expenses incurred and the recording thereof in the appropriate account, to avoid the presentation of misleading information in the Financial Statements.

2.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts due to erroneous recording and likewise record financial transactions in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009, especially in the classification of PPE.

3.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balance of RPT and SET Receivables in the books of accounts with the Certified List of Tax Delinquency prepared by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure accurate financial data presentation in adherence to the provision of Section 20, Chapter 3 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume 1.

4.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor coordinate their efforts in the conduct of regular and massive land tax mapping to cleanse and update their records and come up with accurate list of RPT/SET collectibles. Further, the Municipal Treasurer is required to intensify the collection of delinquent real property taxes that have been long outstanding.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the variance of the reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjustments to arrive at their equal balances. Henceforth, ensure close monitoring of the transactions affecting reciprocal accounts to present fairly the balance of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds in the Statement of Financial Position.

6.10 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive implement the “One-Time Cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, completeness, and accuracy.

7.8 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate subsidiary ledgers of the transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF by year of transfer and prepare necessary adjustments to come up with reconciled balances of Trust Liability- DRRMF in the Statement of Financial Position and unexpended balances of STF in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

8.12 	We recommended that the BAC and TWG apply appropriate method of procurement, meticulously refine the procedures governing the procurement of goods as prescribed in the RA No. 9184 and its IRR, and ensure that transactions are duly supported with complete documentation; as well as the BAC Chairman to apply adequate control and oversight to ensure procurement procedures are in place.

9.8 	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor fully institutionalize the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Office by augmenting the staff for the remaining unfilled LDRRMO positions through any or a combination of the options provided under Item 5.5 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-SCS JMC No. 2014-1, until additional staff are hired, to assure timely implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

10.6	We recommended that the Project In-Charge coordinate with the contractors to facilitate the immediate submission of the lacking documentary requirements of the above-mentioned infrastructure projects, as well as for all future projects. Additionally, the Municipal Accountant verify the completeness of the supporting documents before making any payments in compliance with the aforementioned provisions.

11.5 	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers concerned prioritize the liquidation of fund transfer from DSWD with complete supporting documents in compliance with the above provisions and once the documents are in place, prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the liquidation.

12.7 	We recommended that the BAC Secretariat invite a representative from COA, and at least two (2) observers from a duly recognized private group and non-government organization (NGO), in writing, at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage to ensure transparency of the procurement process.

13.7 	We recommended that the Liquidating Officer apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover her cash accountability. Otherwise, refrain such an officer from exercising the collections of cash.

14.5 	We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other accountable officers regularly prepare and submit monthly Reports of Accountable Forms (RAAF) for all accountable forms, as stipulated in the aforementioned provisions, in order to verify the actual accountabilities of the collectors pertaining to the accountable forms.

We recommended the following to the Management:

a.	The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

b.	The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components.

c.	The Municipal Accountant to completely disclose the local road networks in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements based on the data provided by the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the property, plant and equipment from Trust Fund books to the General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already made the necessary adjusting journal entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to grant cash advance to regular employees of the Municipality only to avoid exposing funds to possible loss. Further, exhaust all possible remedies and demand immediate liquidation of the foregoing unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to attach the approved JEV to all transactions of the LGU, whether cash receipts, cash/check disbursements or non-cash transactions in order to facilitate verification in determining the correctness of the account used.
We recommended that Management submit the required documents for the purchase of the said land pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ascertain that such purchases were valid, legal, and proper.

We recommended that the Management direct the head of implementing units to forward the PPMPs to the MBO first before being sent to BAC for consolidation.  BAC also, thru the BAC Secretariat, consolidates the PPMPs into an indicative APP in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and submit the same to the GPPB including the PMR electronically within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and other concerned offices to submit to the Auditor’s Office the certified copies of the duly signed contracts and purchase orders, respectively, within five working days upon its perfection in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to timely review and verify the transactions and immediately validate the compliance with the terms and conditions stipulated therein.

We recommended that the Supply and/or Property Custodian to accept the delivery of procured items from the supplier before inspection and that the Inspection Officer to inspect the goods in accordance with the required specifications and indicate the date they accepted or inspected. Likewise, the Property and/or Supply Officer notify the Office of the Auditor of the scheduled delivery within 24 hours from acceptance to give the audit team ample time in the conduct of inspection.

We recommended that Management submit justification on the reason of failure to secure the Performance Security from the winning bidder prior to the signing of the contract.

Moreover, Performance Security be required first to the winning bidder before signing of future respective contracts.
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to religiously adhere to the posting requirement of ND at the designated areas of the Municipality.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare and timely submit the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRM duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Office forward the PPMPs to the BAC instead of to the MPDC for consolidation and preparation of an indicative APP. Likewise, submit the APP to the GPPB including the PMR within the prescribed period as required by the relevant provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the IARs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to demand in writing from the concerned agencies on the liquidation/settlement of their fund transfers received for fair
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant review the transactions of reciprocal accounts and effect necessary adjustments in the respective books of accounts to ensure that recording of the financial transactions conforms with generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations as provided for in Section 112 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Prepare and submit the monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format as shown in Annex C of the aforecited LBC;

b)	Post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and

c)	Send a written notice when said reports have been posted on its website to above mentioned government agencies.

We recommended that Management:

a)	Direct the BAC Secretariat to comply with the documentary and posting requirements as provided in the GPPB Circular 01-2020 and require the suppliers to submit a valid warranty clause in each contract or purchase order entered into by the latter; and

b)	Require the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of documentary requirements before the submission of DVs for approval for payment by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended that Management strengthen the application of an effective inventory management system to make sound analysis on the receipt, distribution and/ or utilization of in-kind donations by adhering to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2020-009.

Further, require the concerned offices assigned for the receipt and distribution to ensure that all donations are accounted for and submit immediately a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to COA.

Furthermore, require the Accounting Office and the Property Unit to record timely the in-kind donations in the books or in the registries.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring of goods through MOA. Instead, strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its RIRR that procurement shall be conducted through competitive bidding or any applicable alternative method.

We recommended to stop the charging of programs, projects and activities unrelated to the disaster risk reduction and management activities to the LDRRMF. Instead, the same should be charged to the appropriate fund of the LGU.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive file the request for authority                            to write-off dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or Supervising Auditor (SA) by complying the procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016. Likewise, direct the refund of the excess liquidation to the concerned Accountable Officers.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation and collection of required documents is ongoing. The accountant had already designated a staff to focus on the requirements to write off cash advances.

These personnel who contracted loans from these lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors. Further, ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least ₱5,000.00 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act of 2020.

We recommended that the Municipality execute immediately a binding agreement with BEMCO that in case the lot where the building of the latter was erected will be used by the former in the future, the same should be done at the expense of the latter only.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and other concerned personnel exert all efforts to register all lands owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System in conformity with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Registration of said land titles is still on-going.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order to create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and direct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the necessary status and evaluation reports for submission to DILG.

We recommended that Bids and Award Committee (BAC), thru the BAC Secretariat, submit immediately the lacking documents of the contract and adhere with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management use appropriate source of funds for the repair of computer, purchase of office supplies and materials and wages for construction of MDRRM Office and not utilize the LDRRM Fund to comply NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board ensure that School Improvement Plan was formulated and approved. Further, its formulation is in collaboration with the LGU, the community and stakeholders.

We recommended Management to require the General Service Officer (GSO) and/ or the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on all PPE owned by the LGU and prepare report thereon to be submitted not later than January 31 of each year. Likewise, direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Cards; and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, Real Property Ledger Card, Construction in Progress Ledger Card, Registry of Public Infrastructures and Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Require the Municipal Accountant to recognize depreciation on all depreciable PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management requires the Municipal Accountant to periodically review and analyze all prior years and current year’s transactions of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to effect the necessary adjustments in the respective books of accounts and to record its financial transactions and operations conformably with the generally accepted accounting principles and in accordance with pertinent laws and regulations pursuant to Section 112 of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to fast track the implementation on the eight (8) BUB projects to increase citizens' access to local service delivery as this
was the purpose of the BUB Program.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee that alternative mode of procurement is resorted only upon compliance with RA 9184 and its IRR. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to process the procurement transaction only upon supporting the complete required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate remittance of Barangays shares on RPT and CTC for October to December 2018 and thereafter within 5 days after the end of each quarter in adherence with Section 271 (d) of RA 7160 so that implementation of the PPAs embodied in their Appropriation Ordinances may not be affected.

We recommended the Management direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to transfer all accounts involving PAMANA Program transactions from General Fund Books to the Trust Fund Books by preparing adjusting journal entries. Likewise, furnish the Office of the Auditor of the copies of said journal
entries and its supporting documents.

We recommended that Management:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to use Trust Liabilities- DRRMF account in the Trust Fund Book to record transfers of the LGU’s
unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF from other LGUs and other sources as provided in Section 5.1.12 OF COA Circular No. 2012-002 and make necessary adjustments in the General Fund and Trust Fund books on transactions for calendar year 2017 and below; and to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF using the format in Annex D of same COA Circular.

2. Open a separate depository account for Special; Trust Fund to Deposit the transfer of the Agency’s unutilized QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, receipts of NDRRMF, DRRMF form other LGUs and other sources.

We recommended that Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the source agencies and furnish COA Audit Team copies of it to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for project implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.

We recommended that Management optimally use the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that Management:
a. Compel and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and prepare trial balance and financial statements for all special accounts of the LGU to form part as schedules of the General Fund as provided for in Sections 105, 107 and 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to serve as an effective tool in strategy formulation and decision-making activities of the concerned offices; and
b. Pass a resolution through the Sanggunian Bayan authorizing the LGU for the opening of separate bank accounts for 20% DF and LDRRMF.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive expedite the implementation of projects, programs and activities so that intended recipients of the fund transfers could immediately benefit from it. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Checks Issued and the Report of Disbursement with all supporting vouchers/ payrolls and documents within five (5 days) after the end of each month to the Accountant. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Reports to the Auditor of the LGU and to the Source Agency (SA) (Attention: The SA Accountant) within ten (10) days after receipt from the AO as mentioned in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. Further, we recommend the Management to submit subsidiary ledgers in the quarterly pre-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements and the MOA/TA to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract to evaluate whether the fund is utilized within the agreed stipulations.

We recommended and Management should propose to the Local Chief Executive the immediate relocation of the Solar Pump so that it would be put to good use and resources of the government will not be wasted.

or purchase order entered into by the latter; and

for payment by the Municipal Mayor.

11.5	We recommended that Management require the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the IARs within twenty-four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

12.6	We recommended that Management discontinue the practice of allowing deductions on monthly loan amortizations of government/private lending institutions from employees’ payroll other than those mandated by law. These personnel who contracted loans from these lending institutions must directly remit their payments to the collecting agents/office of these creditors. Further, ensure that each employee receives net take home pay of at least ₱5,000.00 pursuant to the General Appropriations Act of 2020.

a) 	Stop and avoid further payment of the land purchase;

c)	Submit written explanations and/or justifications on why the violations
were committed; and
d)	Ensure that future procurement of land should be in accordance with
the IRR of RA 10752 and Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
1. Stop granting of cash advances for travels unless the District Supervisor is duly appointed of designated disbursing officer and is duly bonded. Otherwise, the LGU may transfer the needed funds of the District Supervisor to the DepEd Surigao del Norte Provincial Division Office to avoid possible loss and/ or misuse of government funds and adhere strictly Item 4.4.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

2. Require the Municipal Accountant’s Office/ Municipal Treasurer’s Office to automatically prepare the disbursement voucher on the remittance of Provincial share for the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF effect the payment thereof within fifteen (15) days of every succeeding month in compliance with Section 9 of RA 5447.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to automatically prepare the disbursement voucher on the remittance of Provincial Share from the additional one percent(1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF; and effect the payment thereof within fifteen (15) days of every succeeding month and remit immediately the unreleased share of P289,982.09 in compliance with Section 9 of RA 5447.

We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC AND Municipal Social Welfare Development Office:
1. To appropriate 1% of the IRA for the LCPC to strengthening and implement its programs; and 

2. To prepare Children Profile and to establish data on children for proper planning and identification of PPAs which is the most need of the children in the Municipality for full delivery of the services as enumerated in section II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.
We recommended that Management manage and immediately utilize the fund efficiently and effectively to ensure that the intended beneficiaries of the BUB projects could timely benefit therefrom.

We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of the PAMANA Program to immediately address the needs of the intended recipients of LGU Bacuag.

We recommended that Management secure copies of the MOA/TA from the source agencies and furnish COA Audit Team copies of it to determine whether fund transfers are properly utilized and the requirements for project implementation and reporting are strictly complied with.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF and fast-track the implementation of the unimplemented development project as of December 31, 2017.

We recommended that Management optimally use the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through the full implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accountable officers concerned strictly comply with the regulations on the liquidation of cash advances as mentioned in               Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Otherwise, failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary as provided for in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the instruction of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.2 and 9.3 thereof. Likewise, failure to liquidate the cash advances in aggregate amounts of Fifty Thousand Pesos (Php50,000.00) and above per accountable officer within the period prescribed shall constitute administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty or Simple Neglect of Duty, as the case may be, pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to reclassify and reconcile the Due from Officers and Employees account to Advances to Special Disbursing Officers and Advance to Officers and Employees with the appropriate amount.

We recommend that Management compel the Municipal Treasurer                 to prepare the RCI daily for each fund and submit the same together with the DVs and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant as provided for in Section 43 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the said report and supporting documents to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided for in Section 39 (3) of P.D. No. 1445 and failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the                         Revised Penal Code.

We recommend that Management cause the automatic suspension of the payment of the accountable officers’ salaries and other emoluments until they shall have complied with the submission of the financial statements and schedules as provided for in Section 41 (3) of PD 1445. Compel the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly and Quarterly                   Pre-Closing Trial Balance not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month duly supported by the Status of Appropriations,  Allotments and Obligations, for both the current and continuing appropriations and supported by a schedule of subsidiary ledger balances of the controlling accounts in the General Ledger; and the Monthly BRS for all depository bank accounts maintained by LGU within ten (10) days                  from receipt of the Bank Statements. Likewise, submit the same together with the Bank Statements and paid checks to the Audit Team. We recommend also that Management enroll with the LANDBANK weAccess which offers a wide range of institutional banking transactions to generate immediate information.

We recommend that Management direct the Solid Waste Management Board to require all Punong Barangays to comply the guidelines and criteria set in Sections 32 and 33 of RA 9003 in the establishment of MRF to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage; and to allow efficient and safe external access and to accommodate internal flow.                          We recommend that Local Chief Executive to close the open dump site, have a sanitary landfill site in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 to protect personnel and public health and safety; and establish and operate the Eco-Solid Waste Management Park based on its description and purpose provided for in NSWMC Resolution No. 79, Series of 2014.
